# ADR 0004: php-vcr Cassette Recordings for Integration Tests

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Integration tests that exercise the full HTTP stack need real API responses. Running them against live Contentful spaces in CI requires test credentials, space IDs, and network access — all of which create flakiness, secret management burden, and cost. Unit mocks, on the other hand, don't exercise actual API response shapes.

## Decision

Use `php-vcr` (with the `covergenius/phpunit-testlistener-vcr` PHPUnit listener) to record HTTP interactions as cassette YAML files in `tests/Recordings/`. Tests replay these cassettes in CI without live credentials. Recording new cassettes requires real credentials and a designated test space; from then on, the cassette is committed to the repo.

Evidence: `phpunit.xml.dist` (VCR listener config), `tests/Recordings/` directory, `require-dev` in `composer.json`.

## Consequences

- CI runs integration tests reliably without live credentials.
- Cassette files must be updated whenever the API request or response shape changes — stale cassettes silently mask bugs.
- A separate E2E test suite (`tests/E2E/`) exists for tests that must run against real spaces (typically run manually or in a privileged CI context).
- New cassette recording requires test-space credentials not available to external contributors — they must request a re-recording from a Contentful team member.
